mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and new housing starts amplifies aggregate demand expectations, leading to higher corporate revenue forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index indicates rising manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of high INDPRO growth periods, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers market repricing of future interest rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered inflation spikes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes directly reflect stronger consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate cash flow expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 for three consecutive years, or forward returns show no statistical difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in over 55% of co-occurring high PAYEMS/low UNRATE months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, which reduces aggregate demand expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of housing start decline periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
